comment_text.sn8.3:2.1: Vaṅgīsa refers to himself by his Vedic clan name of Gotama. The commentary says he was a brahmin, which would explain his training in poetry.
comment_text.sn8.3:5.4: The final verse describes the whole Buddhist path in miniature.

root_text.sn8.3:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā vaṅgīso āḷaviyaṁ viharati aggāḷave cetiye āyasmatā nigrodhakappena upajjhāyena saddhiṁ.
root_text.sn8.3:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā vaṅgīso attano paṭibhānena aññe pesale bhikkhū atimaññati.
root_text.sn8.3:1.4: “alābhā vata me, na vata me lābhā; dulladdhaṁ vata me, na vata me suladdhaṁ;
root_text.sn8.3:1.5: yvāhaṁ attano paṭibhānena aññe pesale bhikkhū atimaññāmī”ti.
root_text.sn8.3:1.6: Atha kho āyasmā vaṅgīso attanāva attano vippaṭisāraṁ uppādetvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imā gāthāyo abhāsi:

translation_text.sn8.3:1.1: At one time Venerable Vaṅgīsa was staying near Āḷavī, at Āḷavī’s premier shrine, together with his mentor, Venerable Nigrodhakappa.
translation_text.sn8.3:1.2: Now at that time Venerable Vaṅgīsa looked down upon other good-hearted mendicants because of his own poetic virtuosity.
translation_text.sn8.3:1.5: that I look down on other good-hearted mendicants because of my own poetic virtuosity.”
translation_text.sn8.3:1.6: Then, on the occasion of arousing remorse in himself, he recited these verses:
translation_text.sn8.3:2.2: Completely abandon the different kinds of conceit!
translation_text.sn8.3:2.3: Besotted with the different kinds of conceit,
translation_text.sn8.3:2.4: you’ve had regrets for a long time.
translation_text.sn8.3:3.1: Smeared by smears and slain by conceit,
translation_text.sn8.3:3.3: When people slain by conceit are reborn in hell,
translation_text.sn8.3:3.4: they grieve for a long time.
translation_text.sn8.3:4.1: But a mendicant who practices rightly,
translation_text.sn8.3:4.3: They enjoy happiness and a good reputation,
translation_text.sn8.3:4.4: and they rightly call him a ‘Seer of Truth’.
translation_text.sn8.3:5.1: So being not hard-hearted, but energetic,
translation_text.sn8.3:5.3: and having completely given up conceit,
translation_text.sn8.3:5.4: the one who makes an end with knowledge is assuaged.”
